item.title: Trump Media, TAE No Longer Pursuing Truth Social Spinoff
item.description: Trump Media & Technology Group, TAE Technologies Inc. and Texas Ventures Acquisition III have decided not to continue pursuing a spinoff of some media assets, including Truth Social.

item.title: Microsoft Has an Answer to Anti-AI Boos From the Class of 2026
item.description: In recent weeks, the Class of 2026 has made its displeasure with AI abundantly clear, loudly booing speakers touting the technology during commencement speeches.

item.title: Argentine Peso Drops Most Over Eight Weeks Since Midterm Selloff
item.description: An incipient decline in the Argentine peso is gaining momentum, as investors grow more risk averse and policymakers in Buenos Aires loosen their grip on the volatile currency.

item.title: CIBC to Offer SpaceX Access Through Canadian Depositary Receipt
item.description: Canadian Imperial Bank of Commerce is set to offer Canadian investors access to SpaceX through a vehicle that gives local investors access to global stocks.

item.title: Bond Traders Keep Bets on a Fed Hike This Year After CPI Report
item.description: Treasuries strengthened slightly after core US inflation accelerated by less than forecast in May, with bond traders maintaining their bets that the Federal Reserve will raise interest rates by the end of the year.

item.title: Boeing Unveils Ghost Bat Upgrades in Pursuit of German Orders
item.description: Boeing Co.’s MQ-28 Ghost Bat uncrewed combat plane can now carry weapons internally, fly farther and deliver a bigger payload — upgrades the company hopes will give it the edge over rival Helsing GmbH in the competition for German orders.

item.title: DCC Inclined to Accept New £5.7 Billion Bid From KKR and ECP
item.description: DCC Plc said it was prepared to accept an improved offer from buyout firms KKR & Co. and Energy Capital Partners, which valued the firm at about £5.7 billion ($7.6 billion).

item.title: Hong Kong Fund Managers Set for Pay Bump in New Tax Plan
item.description: Hedge fund managers in Hong Kong are set to benefit from a change to the city’s tax laws, which will effectively eliminate taxes on performance-related income for some funds.

item.title: US Inflation Accelerates, Though Core Gauge Comes in Softer
item.description: US inflation accelerated in May to the fastest pace in more than three years as the Iran war pushed up energy prices, outstripping Americans’ pay gains.

item.title: SpaceX IPO Draws Billions in Orders From Middle Eastern Funds
item.description: Gulf wealth funds have put in orders for shares worth several billions of dollars in SpaceX’s initial public offering, according to people familiar with the matter, the latest indication of the region’s ambitions to continue playing a lead role in bankrolling the global artificial intelligence buildout.

item.title: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei Has Only One Direct Report
item.description: One of the most powerful AI chief executives has almost no direct reports at a moment when other tech leaders are widening their spans of control.

item.title: Silver Lake’s Lucas Goes Back to School For Lessons in AI
item.description: Christian Lucas, one of the top executives at specialist technology investor Silver Lake, is back in the classroom as he and his firm seek an edge in age of artificial intelligence.

item.title: Amazon’s Trucking Push Sparks Fresh Selloff in Transport Stocks
item.description: Shares of several large trucking companies plunged on Wednesday after Amazon.com Inc. announced an expansion of its shipping service that has already shaken the transportation and logistics sector and unsettled investors.

item.title: Wall Street Braces for SpaceX With Stress Test, ‘Watch Parties’
item.description: Wall Street has spent months debating how much SpaceX is worth. Behind the scenes, a different challenge has occupied the institutions responsible for bringing it public: preparing the plumbing systems needed to support what could become the largest IPO in history.

item.title: EU Budget Leeway for Meloni and Peers Gets Slammed by Watchdog
item.description: The European Commission shouldn’t have given in to pressure from governments seeking extra leeway for energy-related aid, the European Union’s budget watchdog said.

item.title: OpenAI Backs AI Startup Fielding Compliance, Underwriting Tasks
item.description: Poetic, an artificial intelligence startup, is emerging from stealth with $50 million in funding from OpenAI and other investors to help businesses streamline complex tasks, ranging from insurance underwriting to financial compliance.

item.title: US Premarket Movers for June 10, 2026
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures fall 1.1% as of 7:45 a.m. in New York as weakness in technology continues and the US and Iran exchanged strikes overnight.

item.title: Iraq Boosts Oil Exports as More Tankers Transit Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iraq is accelerating oil loadings at its main port and boosting shipments out of the Persian Gulf in the latest sign that the region’s top OPEC producers are getting more barrels through the vital Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: MTN Finalizing Nigeria, Uganda Fintech Spinoffs; Adds Alipay
item.description: MTN Group Ltd. is finalizing the spinoffs of its Nigerian and Ugandan financial-technology operations as part of a reorganization of its mobile-money unit to allow Mastercard Inc. and other strategic buyers to take minority stakes in its fintech businesses.

item.title: China’s AI Ambitions Depend on Massive Power-Grid Investments
item.description: Beijing’s $295 billion data center rollout will need to be supported by continued spending on electricity generation and networks.

item.title: Costs to Hedge the $9 Trillion S&P 500 Rally Jump Ahead of Fed
item.description: Sentiment in the US stock market has shifted quickly from fear of missing out to fear of getting wiped out.

item.title: Credit Agricole CEO Says AI Anxiety Must Come to an End
item.description: Credit Agricole SA Chief Executive Officer Olivier Gavalda is trying to temper worries related to artificial intelligence in the banking sector, as many executives have said implementing such technology will lead to workforce reductions.

item.title: US Annual Inflation Is Highest in Over Three Years
item.description: Shoppers during the grand opening of an Olive Young store in Pasadena, California.

item.title: Bitcoin Selloff Leaves Half of All Supply Trading at a Loss
item.description: Bitcoin’s latest retreat has been so severe, it has pushed more than half its circulating supply into loss-making territory.

item.title: AI Firms’ Explosive Growth Improves the Math for Late-Stage Investors
item.description: Later investments are producing much higher returns than in the past

item.title: Cosco Widens Cargo Beachhead in Spain as Car Imports Rise
item.description: Cosco has been awarded a 50-year port concession in Tarragona on the east coast

item.title: Jevons Paradox Gains Traction in Debate Over AI Job Impact
item.description: An 1860s theory is revived to explain the effects of a new technology on the labor market.

item.title: Thailand, Malaysia Clash Over Seafood Trade Curbs
item.description: A trade dispute between Malaysia and Thailand is escalating, with the latter saying it may raise the issue at the World Trade Organization.

item.title: Ghana Economy Grows 6.4% as Reforms Offset Iran War Impact
item.description: Ghana’s economy expanded at a faster pace in the first quarter as companies benefited from lower inflation and interest rates, with reforms helping offset the impact from the war in Iran.

item.title: All the Ways Wall Street Gets Paid to Fund AI Boom
item.description: Enormous valuations are rewriting the IPO playbook.
